ops.title: SecureEnclaveWrapper.Close ignores cache-dropkey exit code
ops.description: Pre-existing, low severity (flagged in the v0.10.4 round-5 review, out of scope there). In internal/cache/cache.go, SecureEnclaveWrapper.Close only checks the Go error from helper.Bridge.CacheDropkey, not result.Code — so a clean nonzero cache-dropkey exit (a genuine helper-level drop failure, as opposed to an exec error) is treated as a successful cleanup. Combined with healingWrapper.Close now clearing se only on a nil error (v0.10.4), a Code-based failure would still nil se and report success while the Enclave key stays live. Fix: check result.Code (nonzero => error) in SecureEnclaveWrapper.Close, mirroring how heal treats CacheNewkey's result.Code, so a failed drop is surfaced and (per v0.10.4's Close) left retryable.

ops.text: a nonzero cache-dropkey exit surfaces as an error from SecureEnclaveWrapper.Close (not a silent success)
